required_complexity_patterns.notes: Newsletter or login decoy form separate from job application form
At least one readonly or disabled input with prefilled profile value
Yes/no question as paired button elements, not native radio inputs
At least two fields with aria-label only and no visible label element
Fields inside a shadow DOM host or declarative shadow root

prompt_summary: Create a messy, production-real ashby-style job application (single-page, small, tier=high). Widgets: combobox. Required: Newsletter or login decoy form separate from job application form Required: At least one readonly or disabled input with prefilled profile value Required: Yes/no question as paired button elements, not native radio inputs Required: At least two fields with aria-label only and no visible label element Required: Fields inside a shadow DOM host or declarative shadow root Reference DOM (Aria describedby hints): Fields use aria-describedby for format hints separate from label Reference DOM (Micro1 Q label pattern): micro1-inspired step-2 labels like Q2.full name with dot separator Reference DOM (Other option reveal): Referral source Other checkbox reveals free-text input Do NOT produce a tutorial-style form where every field is label[for]+native input. Mix native controls with non-semantic production DOM from the patterns below. Implement ALL required complexity patterns: decoy-form, readonly-prefill, pill-yes-no, aria-label-only, shadow-host. Include at least one custom widget (div role=textbox, pill buttons, or listbox combobox). Include at least one labeling strategy besides label-for (aria-label, aria-labelledby, glued spans, placeholder-only). Layout: sidebar + main split apply layout. Avoid generic centered SaaS card as the only structure. High tier: include shadow DOM host OR iframe apply shell OR wizard/conditional reveal with hidden fields. Mirror structural messiness like these references: Aria describedby hints; Micro1 Q label pattern; Other option reveal.
